confidenceReason: The twelve most recent tracker handoffs were read directly.
evidenceSummary: Recent handoffs state goals, but most omit done criteria and the confirming sensor.

basis.statement: The handoff sample was read in full; the omissions are countable.

findings.condition: Nine of the twelve most recent handoffs state a goal but no done criteria; none name the sensor that would confirm completion.

criteria.criterion: Sampled handoffs state goals, non-goals, done criteria, and the sensor that confirms completion.
criteria.rationale: Without done criteria an agent must guess when work is finished.

effect.statement: Agents declare completion on judgment rather than criteria, which constrains task specifiability to minimum.

evidence.statement: The sampled handoffs contain goal statements; done criteria appear in three, a confirming sensor in none.

findings.statement: Most quality-loop handoffs omit done criteria and the confirming sensor.

summary: Recent handoffs state goals, but most omit done criteria and the confirming sensor.
